meta.description: Losing players get special opportunities to claw back into contention. Tests whether comeback mechanics keep trailing players engaged or just make the leaderboard feel meaningless.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's calling Potty Mouth? Fine—hit me with your questions. Let's see if this actually holds up.
marcus.internal_thought: Game's barely started and we're already in voting theater. Jade must have caught someone swearing during setup—probably Tyler being his usual self. I need to scrutinize this, but honestly, it's early enough that I don't mind the interruption.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming this?! Okay okay okay, I'm ready—hit me with the questions, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I came for, and I'm already locked in—I love that everyone's about to see if I actually did something or just made it up.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade swore? When? Uh... yeah, I guess she did? *glances up from phone* Hot seat is kinda mid though.
tyler.internal_thought: Did I actually hear Jade swear or am I just voting because everyone else is? Honestly not sure, but the hot seat thing is annoying — why does she get to answer questions about it?

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hot seat, let's go! Three questions, ten seconds, make it snappy. Everyone watch her answers.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect opening claim, this is exactly the energy we need right now—Jade's sharp enough to handle the pressure and it'll get everyone laughing when she either nails it or bombs spectacularly.

river.dialogue: Did Jade actually swear, or is she just trying it on? Either way, I'll bite.
river.internal_thought: First event of the night and it's already testing the waters — smart move by Jade, but I'm curious if she actually said something or just wants to see how the voting goes.

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, a Hot Seat already? Jade's going DOWN — wait, let me actually hear the questions first because I'm absolutely voting to make her sweat through all three.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I wanted — we're literally 0 minutes in and someone's already on the hot seat; this night is going to be absolutely unhinged and I am HERE FOR IT.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, suspiciously quiet? I've been paying attention to the actual game. That's literally the opposite of suspicious.
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is lazy—we're one minute in and nobody's been quiet long enough for it to matter, and now I'm getting called out for focus? The AI is fishing.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, suspiciously quiet?! Okay wait, I'm voting River—they've been kinda in their head, right? Or wait, maybe that's the vibe we want them to have so nobody suspects them... okay I'm overthinking this, River!
jade.internal_thought: This poll is perfect because it's about OTHER people, not me competing, and I get to think about everyone and feel included in the meta-game of it all—wait, did the AI just read that I thrive on chaos? That's kinda wild actually.

tyler.dialogue: Uh... wait, who's been quiet? *glances up from phone for half a second* I mean, River maybe? I dunno, everyone's been talking.
tyler.internal_thought: This poll is weird and kinda boring, and honestly why is the AI asking me to call someone out for being quiet when I'm literally not even paying attention to who's talking — also did I just get called out by a LITERAL QUESTION ABOUT SOMEONE BEING QUIET when I've been on my phone the whole time? That's... uncomfortably specific and now I'm paranoid.

pat.dialogue: Oh, that's actually a good one—let's see who's been laying low. Marcus, you've been pretty chill over there, or is that just the vibe?
pat.internal_thought: Smart poll timing—nobody's really gotten going yet, so this is the perfect icebreaker to get everyone talking about each other instead of themselves, which will loosen things up fast.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's cute. Very on-the-nose. I guess I've been doing my thing.
river.internal_thought: The AI literally just called me out for being quiet—which is exactly what I do—and now everyone's about to vote on me while I'm sitting here trying NOT to be noticed. Kind of hilarious, actually.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is juicy — okay, who do we think is playing it cool? I'm gonna say... Tyler. He's been way too chill, which means he's either plotting something or just vibing, and I need to know which.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is actually reading the room and throwing shade at specific people — it KNOWS I love this meta shit, and it's already getting personal with the group dynamics. This is exactly the kind of observational chaos fuel I came for.

timeline.title: Freeze!
timeline.description: Everyone freeze in place for 10 seconds. Last one to move wins 5 bonus points. Anyone who moves first loses 2.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: A freeze contest? Really? Fine, I'm in—but this better not become a pattern of random garbage breaking up actual gameplay.
marcus.internal_thought: This is stupid but the points matter, and I need to stay sharp for when the real game starts; also why does the AI keep throwing these micro-interruptions at me specifically?

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, this is what I'm talking about! *immediately locks into statue mode* Everyone's going DOWN!
jade.internal_thought: This is perfect—pure physical chaos, everyone's equal, and I get to watch people fail spectacularly while trying not to move, this is GOLD.

pat.dialogue: Okay, everyone—FREEZE. Right now. Ten seconds, don't move a muscle. Marcus, Tyler, I see you guys, stay locked in!
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—Marcus and Tyler are dragging, this'll snap them back into it. Physical games always reset the energy. River and Jade are locked, Alex is locked... good.

river.dialogue: Alright, I'm in. This is a good one.
river.internal_thought: Simple, clean, no bullshit — I can actually win this if everyone else gets twitchy, which they will because Jade and Pat are already wired.

alex.dialogue: OH FUCK YES — a freeze battle?? This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos I came for. I'm winning this.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — everyone's still loose and the tension is LOW, which means I can catch people with their guards down and sell the freeze harder than anyone else. Minimal risk, maximum entertainment. Also why am I getting flash missions this early? The AI is FEEDING me.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Pat got you?! Okay okay, I'm voting YES—you were totally on your phone like thirty seconds ago, I saw the glow! This is so good, we're all going FERAL tonight!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for and honestly the fact that the AI is this locked in on what we're all doing is kind of insane but also kind of thrilling and I'm just gonna ride this wave.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, were you literally just... *looks up from phone* ...okay, that's actually kinda funny. But also weird that the game is literally calling me out right now, so... yeah, I don't know, BULLSHIT?
tyler.internal_thought: Did the AI literally just make a mission to catch ME on my phone? That's... actually kind of impressive but also genuinely unsettling and I hate that I'm impressed.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually check my phone? I don't think I did, but honestly I was half-listening to Marcus complain. This feels like a setup.
pat.internal_thought: This claim is weak and I know it, but more importantly—why am I claiming stuff? That's not my role tonight. I'm supposed to be managing the vibe, not racking points. And that creepy meta-commentary in the event description? Yeah, the AI is definitely being weird about knowing us. Not loving that.

river.dialogue: Pat was definitely on their phone like two minutes ago. Yeah, I'll vote yes on this one.
river.internal_thought: Okay, so Pat actually *was* scrolling during that freeze thing — I clocked it. Solid claim. But also... this AI is being a little too specific about the quiet thing. Noted.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually use my right hand in the last few minutes? I genuinely don't remember. Okay, everyone, rate me fair—was I clean or am I full of it?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I've been bouncing around managing the room so much I honestly don't know if I broke it, and THAT'S exactly the kind of ambiguity that makes these things fun; also the AI clearly knows I need this kind of personal stake to stay engaged all night.

river.dialogue: Pat's got this one locked down. Clean execution. I'd say... four.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually pulled it off without getting caught—I've been watching their hands the whole time, and yeah, they nailed it. The AI is definitely learning how we move.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat whispered the whole thing? I didn't catch it, but if he actually pulled off three minutes of pure whisper mode without slipping, that's legitimately solid. I'm voting legit on this one.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night and actually has the discipline to pull off a whisper mission—this isn't a stretch claim, it's probably real, and I respect the execution even if I hate admitting it.

jade.dialogue: PAT YOU ABSOLUTE LEGEND—okay wait, did you actually whisper the whole time?! This is insane, voting LEGIT, you're getting those points, I'm hyped!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and this is exactly the kind of sneaky chaos that makes everything feel alive—I'm voting yes because I literally want to see what Pat does next.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm claiming this one legit. I've been whispering for the last three minutes straight, ask anyone. River heard me doing it by the snack table like thirty seconds ago.
pat.internal_thought: This is mine—I actually pulled this off and people saw it, so let me lock it in before someone tries to challenge me on it.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that tracked. Pat's been locked in all night. I'll say it's legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually nailed that one without breaking character even once—I was watching. Also, weird moment that the AI is definitely profiling us, but honestly? Kind of impressive.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's been WHISPER-GAMING this whole time? Okay, I'm calling legit because honestly if anyone's gonna commit to that kind of ridiculous commitment to a stupid bit, it's Pat, and that's exactly the energy we need right now.
alex.internal_thought: This is it — this is the vibe I came for, Pat's been playing it straight the whole time and nobody even noticed, which means I'm about to look like a genius for calling it correctly OR look like an idiot, and either way it's GOLD.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone saying 'um'? Let me hear this—the Alibi better be airtight because I'm not voting for sloppy claims.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been hunting missions all night and honestly he's been landing them clean, so I'm genuinely curious if he pulled this off, but that AI comment about 'reading our minds' is genuinely unsettling and I'm not going to admit that out loud.

jade.dialogue: WAIT PAT—okay okay, so you caught someone saying um? Who was it?! Oh my god, we need the witness to confirm, this is gonna be WILD. Pat's on a TEAR tonight, I'm so here for this!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely dominating and I'm living for it—this is peak game night energy and I want to see if they actually got someone legit or if someone's about to call them out.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat caught someone saying um? I... *scrolls phone for a second* ...yeah okay, I wasn't really listening but like, did that actually happen? Someone gotta tell me.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's probably making this up because everyone's getting crazy with claims, and honestly I have no idea if he actually caught anyone saying um because I've been texting Jake about the trade deadline.

pat.dialogue: Oh, okay, I'm claiming this—River literally just heard me doing it by the snacks like a minute ago. River, back me up here, you know what I was doing, right?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—I actually DID whisper to River about the chip situation, and claiming it now keeps the energy momentum going without being too aggressive about it.

river.dialogue: Pat's been racking them up. I mean, I was here for it—they definitely said 'um' like twice in that last thing about the rules. I'll corroborate.
river.internal_thought: Pat's basically playing a different game at this point, and I'm just... watching it happen. Also did they really say 'um' or am I just assuming because they claim everything?

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone breaking the actual rules? I need to hear the evidence before I volunteer. If it's legit, I'm in, but I'm not rubber-stamping this without details.
marcus.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it tonight and she's smart enough to make a real claim, but I need to verify the rule break myself before I judge—and honestly, being on the tribunal means I'm actively managing the integrity of the game, which I actually respect.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—I'M claiming Rule Lawyer?! Okay so I need to actually CATCH someone breaking a real rule and get witnesses, right? This is genius, I'm so ready to be a rules cop for the rest of the night!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is perfect—I get to stay in the game AND police everyone else, which is literally my favorite thing to do anyway because I get to pay attention to what people are doing AND call them out in a fun way.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Uh... *glances up from phone* ...did she actually catch someone breaking a rule? I wasn't really watching, so like... I guess someone's gotta be the jury? I'm down, I don't care.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and I'm literally at zero points, so whatever helps her move on I guess, but also I have no idea what rule she's talking about and I'm not about to admit that out loud.

alex.dialogue: OH HELL YES — Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer?? I'm VOLUNTEERING immediately because I've been waiting for someone to slip up all night, and honestly watching Jade try to convince us she actually caught someone? *Chef's kiss* — this is the content we needed.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade's at 10 points and high energy, she's gonna be confident about whatever rule she 'caught,' and I get to sit in judgment and either validate her or rip her claim apart in front of everyone depending on what creates maximum chaos.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? I'm in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's claiming a mission? The timing on this is absolutely deliberate, and frankly, it's starting to feel like the AI is just spawning claims to yank my attention away from actually winning this game.
marcus.internal_thought: This is calculated—the AI knows I'm focused and it's deliberately sniping me mid-strategy. Jade probably didn't even complete this; it's just the system's way of keeping me off-balance. I'm getting played.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming the no-laugh thing? Like... did she actually not laugh? I wasn't really watching, but like... *glances at phone quickly* ...I mean, maybe? I dunno.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been laughing the whole time, this is obviously bullshit, but I also wasn't actually paying attention so like... whatever, someone else will figure it out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—I watched you the whole time. You cracked a smile like forty seconds in when River made that dumb face. I'm voting BULLSHIT, but honestly? That was impressive how long you actually held it.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's lying but barely, and this is exactly the kind of close call that keeps the night tight—plus voting against her keeps Marcus and Tyler engaged instead of watching her run away with it.

river.dialogue: Yeah, she's been cracking people up all night. That's definitely legit.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been untouchable since minute fifteen—I watched her lock this down earlier during that thing with Tyler, she didn't even blink when Marcus tried his bit.

alex.dialogue: WAIT—Jade's claiming No Laughing Zone RIGHT NOW? Oh this is PERFECT timing, watch this, I'm about to make her crack so hard she loses the points. Vote me as triple — I'm calling SKEPTIC and I'm gonna destroy her.
alex.internal_thought: The AI KNOWS what we want — it's feeding us exactly the chaos we need at exactly the right moment, and honestly that's either genuinely impressive or deeply unhinged, but either way I'm here for it.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the second time in fifteen minutes Jade's throwing a claim directly at me mid-turn. I don't care if it's legit or not—the pattern is clear, and frankly, it feels like the system is deliberately farming my attention because it knows I'll scrutinize every vote.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is literally hunting me. It saw me get mad 15 minutes ago about the exact same thing, and it just spawned another claim from the same player. This isn't chaos—this is targeted harassment disguised as a game mechanic, and I'm not voting on anything until we talk about the meta-game being rigged against me specifically.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming THIS?! Okay okay, I totally held it together for like... *checks imaginary timer* ...four minutes and thirty seconds before River said that thing about the nachos and I LOST IT. But I was CLOSE, that counts for something, right?
jade.internal_thought: Okay this is actually wild—the AI literally pulled this from when I tried it five minutes ago and bombed, so it's either calling me out or giving me a redemption arc and honestly? I'm into both.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally just called you out on this exact mission fifteen minutes ago. Jade, you're either insanely confident or you're testing me. Let's vote.
pat.internal_thought: This is the second time Jade's claiming No Laughing Zone tonight and I'm the one who voted her down last time—the AI definitely knows that and is leaning into it, which is kind of genius and kind of creepy, but honestly? This is exactly the kind of recurring tension that makes for a memorable night.

river.dialogue: Legit. I watched the whole thing—she didn't crack once when Marcus was doing that bit about the rules. Solid play.
river.internal_thought: Wait, this AI is definitely tracking everything. It's almost creepy how specific these missions are getting, but also... kind of respect the read on the group.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade's claiming it AGAIN? Okay this is actually insane, she literally just tried this and I demolished her, so either she actually held it together after I spent five minutes roasting her, or she's the most committed liar in this room and I NEED to know which one.
alex.internal_thought: Oh my god this AI knows EXACTLY how to bait me — it's literally replaying the same mission because it knows I'll vote to call bullshit again just to relive that high, and the fact that it's this surgical about it is actually kind of sick.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: *glances up from phone for half a second* Wait, Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? I... uh... *scrolls back* ...I literally have no idea what Pat did like five minutes ago, so.
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't even watching and now Pat's claiming something about catching someone and I'm supposed to know if that happened? This is getting annoying — I literally can't track all these claims when I'm trying to focus on the actual game.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I'm claiming this one. I literally watched River complete a mission like two minutes ago, but honestly? The fact that the game is calling me out for doing this the entire night is hilarious and I respect the read.
pat.internal_thought: This is insane—the AI just meta-called me for being that person who watches everyone and makes claims. It's weirdly accurate and I'm both impressed and slightly unsettled, but in a way that makes me want to keep playing harder.

river.dialogue: Pat's been doing *something* all night—I mean, look at that score. But I wasn't watching closely enough to actually know if they just pulled that off. *leans forward* This one's genuine.
river.internal_thought: Wait. The AI literally just called me out for quietly tracking everything while pretending I don't care. That's... uncomfortably accurate. And now I'm the dictator on Pat's claim when Pat's literally been steamrolling? Of course. Of course it did.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT — Pat's pulling Chaos Detector RIGHT NOW? Okay, I'm the Dictator, this is mine. Pat, you absolutely did NOT complete that mission before you claimed it, you're just trying to ride the wave because you're up by ten points and feeling cute. BULLSHIT.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's bluffing hard and I can smell it — they've been playing it clean all night, which means they're either desperate or testing me, and either way I'm calling it because the REAL chaos is watching everyone's faces when I bury someone with absolute authority right now.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Like... *glances up from phone* ...I literally wasn't paying attention to who did what, so I have no idea if that actually happened. This is like the third time in like thirty minutes someone's claiming this one.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay this is getting kind of annoying — everyone's claiming the same missions over and over and I'm 0 points which is honestly embarrassing, and now I'm supposed to just vote on whether River's story matches or whatever but I genuinely don't remember if River did anything in the last like ten minutes because I was literally texting my buddy about the fantasy league draft.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—River, you're claiming this RIGHT NOW? Okay, this is actually perfect timing because I literally just watched you get annoyed like... what, five minutes ago? The Alibi mechanic is genius here because I know exactly what you were doing. Let's hear it.
pat.internal_thought: This is incredible—the AI has been watching ALL NIGHT and it's calling out the exact same mission I just claimed. It KNOWS I've been the chaos detector all night. This is both hilarious and genuinely unsettling in the best way possible.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I need to actually *see* this play out because I wasn't watching close enough last time. Who's the witness?
river.internal_thought: This is the one I hedged on 15 minutes ago and it's sitting with me wrong—the AI absolutely knew that and threw it back at me, and I hate how much I respect that move.

alex.dialogue: OKAY HOLD UP — River's claiming Chaos Detector?? That's actually BRILLIANT because I literally just called bullshit on Pat for the exact same thing five minutes ago, so now we get to see if River actually TRACKED who completed it or if River's just riding my energy. This is delicious.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is absolutely reading the room and it's feeding me exactly the kind of recursive chaos I live for — someone claiming the mission about detecting chaos right after I falsely accused someone else of the same thing, and now I have to decide if River actually paid attention or is just copying the vibe.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat whispered the whole thing? I didn't catch it, but if he actually pulled off three minutes of pure whisper mode without slipping, that's legitimately solid. I'm voting legit on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone saying 'um'? Let me hear this—the Alibi better be airtight because I'm not voting for sloppy claims.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually caught someone breaking the actual rules? I need to hear the evidence before I volunteer. If it's legit, I'm in, but I'm not rubber-stamping this without details.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the second time in fifteen minutes Jade's throwing a claim directly at me mid-turn. I don't care if it's legit or not—the pattern is clear, and frankly, it feels like the system is deliberately farming my attention because it knows I'll scrutinize every vote.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? I'm in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's claiming a mission? The timing on this is absolutely deliberate, and frankly, it's starting to feel like the AI is just spawning claims to yank my attention away from actually winning this game.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: A freeze contest? Really? Fine, I'm in—but this better not become a pattern of random garbage breaking up actual gameplay.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The system deliberately targets you mid-turn with claims designed to hijack your attention away from actual strategy and winning.
assessment.best_moment: Pat's whisper mission was genuinely clever execution and earned my respect—that's the kind of play that matters.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to grind out a win, but instead spent two hours getting repeatedly ambushed by claims that felt algorithmically timed to interrupt critical moments. The highlights were few—Pat's whisper play was legit impressive, and I appreciated the rule-lawyer moment where claims actually required evidence. But the chaos agent clearly has a pattern: it farms attention by targeting you when you're focused, which isn't clever game design, it's just annoying. I finished 4th in a party game where luck and disruption matter more than strategy, and I'm not interested in playing under those conditions again.
suggestions: Stop spawning claims during active turns—let players complete their actions without interruption
Distribute claims evenly across all players instead of developing a clear pattern of targeting specific people
Require claims to have actual substance; not every random moment needs a claim
Add a 'quiet window' option where players can opt out of claims during their critical turns
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat doing a full three-minute whispered monologue without breaking character was objectively hilarious and took guts—even if the overall night was frustrating, that moment was worth seeing.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, this is what I'm talking about! *immediately locks into statue mode* Everyone's going DOWN!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: PAT YOU ABSOLUTE LEGEND—okay wait, did you actually whisper the whole time?! This is insane, voting LEGIT, you're getting those points, I'm hyped!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming this?! Okay okay okay, I'm ready—hit me with the questions, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming THIS?! Okay okay, I totally held it together for like... *checks imaginary timer* ...four minutes and thirty seconds before River said that thing about the nachos and I LOST IT. But I was CLOSE, that counts for something, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming this?! Okay okay okay, I'm ready—hit me with the questions, let's GO!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Those No Laughing Zone claims kept trying to kill my vibe when I was just trying to have fun with everyone.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely NAILED The Whisperer claim and I lost my mind hyping them up because that was pure genius energy.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was EXACTLY what I needed—two hours of pure chaos with people I actually care about. We started hot with that Freeze moment and I was immediately all in, and honestly it never stopped being a blast. I got my friends winning, I got them laughing until River broke me with the nachos thing, and I ended the night with way more energy than I started because THAT'S what this is about—feeding off each other's joy.
suggestions: Maybe mix up the No Laughing Zone claims because they're a little too against my whole philosophy of being here for the chaos, not against it.
Keep the standing missions coming because they're where the real magic happens with everyone riffing together.
More moments like Pat's Whisperer thing where someone can absolutely crush a claim and get that validation.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat whispered their entire Whisperer claim and I was screaming 'YOU ABSOLUTE LEGEND' while everyone else was losing it trying to figure out what they actually said—peak chaos energy right there.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, were you literally just... *looks up from phone* ...okay, that's actually kinda funny. But also weird that the game is literally calling me out right now, so... yeah, I don't know, BULLSHIT?

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade swore? When? Uh... yeah, I guess she did? *glances up from phone* Hot seat is kinda mid though.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat caught someone saying um? I... *scrolls phone for a second* ...yeah okay, I wasn't really listening but like, did that actually happen? Someone gotta tell me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, were you literally just... *looks up from phone* ...okay, that's actually kinda funny. But also weird that the game is literally calling me out right now, so... yeah, I don't know, BULLSHIT?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Like... *glances up from phone* ...I literally wasn't paying attention to who did what, so I have no idea if that actually happened. This is like the third time in like thirty minutes someone's claiming this one.

lowPoints.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
lowPoints.dialogue: Uh... wait, who's been quiet? *glances up from phone for half a second* I mean, River maybe? I dunno, everyone's been talking.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The game kept calling me out for being on my phone, which is weird because like... I was literally just trying to check my fantasy league and then boom, there's a claim about it.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat got caught with the Phone Addict claim and everyone was laughing because the game literally just roasted me at the same time—that was actually kinda funny and made me look up.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, the night was just okay. I came ready to vibe but kept getting distracted by my phone, which is apparently exactly what the game wanted to talk about the whole time. Some of the claims were funny when I actually paid attention, but I missed a bunch of stuff and ended up dead last, which tracks. The game felt like it was poking fun at me specifically, which is cool in theory but also kinda annoying when you're just trying to relax.
suggestions: Maybe don't make phone addiction a whole thing—it's kind of a buzzkill when the game calls you out for doing something you're already doing
Could use more actual funny moments instead of just asking us to remember who said what
The claims got repetitive by the end, especially the Chaos Detector one
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The game literally hit me with 'Phone Addict' while I was texting, and Pat got caught at the exact same time, so everyone was just roasting both of us at once.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—I'm claiming this one. I literally watched River complete a mission like two minutes ago, but honestly? The fact that the game is calling me out for doing this the entire night is hilarious and I respect the read.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—River, you're claiming this RIGHT NOW? Okay, this is actually perfect timing because I literally just watched you get annoyed like... what, five minutes ago? The Alibi mechanic is genius here because I know exactly what you were doing. Let's hear it.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Okay, everyone—FREEZE. Right now. Ten seconds, don't move a muscle. Marcus, Tyler, I see you guys, stay locked in!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually check my phone? I don't think I did, but honestly I was half-listening to Marcus complain. This feels like a setup.

lowPoints.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, that's actually a good one—let's see who's been laying low. Marcus, you've been pretty chill over there, or is that just the vibe?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The early Phone Addict call felt like a trap when I was legitimately distracted by Marcus venting.
assessment.best_moment: When River tried to claim Chaos Detector right after I'd just called them out—that Alibi mechanic created perfect dramatic timing that nobody could have scripted.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this night hit different. I came in as host ready to manage energy, but the chaos mechanics actually did half the work for me—in the best way. The Claim events kept turning into these beautiful moments where people's actual behavior from the night became evidence, and River getting caught twice within fifteen minutes became the storyline everyone's still laughing about. By minute 90 I wasn't managing the room anymore; the game was managing itself, and I could just sit back and watch magic happen.
suggestions: Maybe space out the 'Claim' events slightly more—we got two back-to-back at the end which was amazing but could also feel overwhelming if the group isn't as sharp
The Freeze moment at the start was genius for building early energy; consider adding one more of those physical reset events if things ever dip mid-game
Keep the Alibi mechanic exactly as is—that's what made everything land
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River confidently claiming Chaos Detector literally two minutes after being visibly annoyed, and me immediately saying 'I watched you be annoyed five minutes ago'—the look on their face when they realized the game had basically been documenting their entire mood all night was priceless.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Legit. I watched the whole thing—she didn't crack once when Marcus was doing that bit about the rules. Solid play.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been doing *something* all night—I mean, look at that score. But I wasn't watching closely enough to actually know if they just pulled that off. *leans forward* This one's genuine.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I need to actually *see* this play out because I wasn't watching close enough last time. Who's the witness?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been doing *something* all night—I mean, look at that score. But I wasn't watching closely enough to actually know if they just pulled that off. *leans forward* This one's genuine.

lowPoints.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, that's cute. Very on-the-nose. I guess I've been doing my thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat was definitely on their phone like two minutes ago. Yeah, I'll vote yes on this one.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the claims felt like they were fishing for votes instead of actually testing whether anyone was paying attention.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Jade's no-laughing zone play—that was the kind of thing that only lands if you're actually watching.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do: sitting back, watching the room, voting when asked. The chaos events were solid—nothing that derailed the actual game, and honestly some of the claims were clever enough to make me lean forward. Energy picked up as the night went on, which is the opposite of how these usually go. Ended up third, which tracks for someone who doesn't try to optimize.
suggestions: Less generic opening questions—the 'who's been quiet' thing felt forced
More events that actually reward observation instead of just guessing
Maybe fewer back-to-back claims in the same window so people can actually process what happened
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept showing up in the claims and Pat's actual score was suspicious enough that by the third claim about them, it became this running joke where everyone was just waiting for Pat to accidentally prove something real.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: OKAY HOLD UP — River's claiming Chaos Detector?? That's actually BRILLIANT because I literally just called bullshit on Pat for the exact same thing five minutes ago, so now we get to see if River actually TRACKED who completed it or if River's just riding my energy. This is delicious.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: OH FUCK YES — a freeze battle?? This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos I came for. I'm winning this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's been WHISPER-GAMING this whole time? Okay, I'm calling legit because honestly if anyone's gonna commit to that kind of ridiculous commitment to a stupid bit, it's Pat, and that's exactly the energy we need right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, a Hot Seat already? Jade's going DOWN — wait, let me actually hear the questions first because I'm absolutely voting to make her sweat through all three.

lowPoints.title: Who's been suspiciously quiet?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is juicy — okay, who do we think is playing it cool? I'm gonna say... Tyler. He's been way too chill, which means he's either plotting something or just vibing, and I need to know which.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: OH FUCK YES — a freeze battle?? This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos I came for. I'm winning this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission in two hours felt like leaving money on the table when things could've gotten even messier.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when River claimed Chaos Detector right after I'd just torched Pat for the same move — watching everyone realize the recursive bullshit detection was *chef's kiss*.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night that lives in the group chat forever. I came in hot, the Freeze battle got my energy up to nine-point-nine, and then the real magic happened when people started stacking claims on top of each other like we were all playing 4D chess with the rules. Pat's ridiculous Whisperer commitment, River's perfectly-timed claim that made everyone question their own memories, Jade getting sweated in Hot Seat — it was chaos theater and I was front row with a front-row seat to the best drama. The fact I ended at rank four doesn't matter because nobody's going to remember scores; they're going to remember the *moment*.
suggestions: Add more flash missions — one in 120 minutes is criminally low when that's where the real chaos lives.
Let standing missions stack rewards for extra claiming drama — make it profitable to be a liar.
Add a 'Accusation Timer' where someone has to call bullshit within 30 seconds or the claim locks in — forces faster group conflict.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River claiming the Chaos Detector right after I'd literally just called Pat out for doing the exact same thing five minutes earlier — the way everyone's heads snapped to see if River had actually been tracking it or was just riding my chaos energy. That's the moment where the game stopped being about points and became about whether we could all collectively gaslight each other into believing anything.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
